title: Future Context with Present Tense
description: Using present tense for future events with context words like 'mañana' and 'pronto'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: We can talk about the future using the present tense with future context words. For example, how do you say 'tomorrow'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Mañana. Good. How about 'later' or 'more late'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Más tarde. And 'tonight' or 'this night'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Esta noche. Good. Another word is 'pronto'. What does 'pronto' mean?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Soon. You might hear 'get here pronto' in English, meaning 'get here soon'. Now, what is the verb 'to see'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ver. How do you say 'he sees'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ve. Good. How about 'they see'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ven. And 'you (informal) see'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ves. How do you say 'we see'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vemos. The 'I' form is a bit irregular: it's 'veo'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Veo. How would you say 'I see her later'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: La veo más tarde. How about 'I'm seeing her tonight'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: La veo esta noche. How do you say 'we are seeing her soon'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: La vemos pronto. If we mean a female group, how do you say 'we're seeing them soon'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Las vemos pronto. How would you say 'he is seeing them soon'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Las ve pronto. And 'you (informal) are seeing them soon'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Las ves pronto. How would you ask 'are you seeing them soon?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Las ves pronto? And 'why aren't you seeing them soon?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Por qué no las ves pronto? Now, let's use 'going to' for future. How would you say 'we're going to see her'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How about 'are you going to see him soon?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Vas a verlo pronto? How would you say 'are you going to see us soon?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Vas a vernos pronto? Or you could say, ¿Nos vas a ver pronto? How about 'why aren't you going to see us soon?'

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: ¿por qué no vas a vernos pronto?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Por qué no vas a vernos pronto? Now, how do you say 'next week' using 'the week that comes'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: La semana que viene. How would you say 'let's speak next week'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hablamos la semana que viene. How do you say 'we see each other'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Nos vemos. This is a common way to say goodbye in Spanish. How would you say 'we will see each other next week'?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: nos vemos la semana que viene

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Nos vemos la semana que viene. Remember, using present tense with future context allows us to talk about future events.

outro.text: You've learned how to use present tense with future context words to talk about future events.
Try using 'mañana', 'pronto', or 'la semana que viene' in your own sentences to describe future plans.
Next time, we'll explore more ways to express future actions in Spanish.
